evals.prompt: Configure this pnpm monorepo as a Solo project with Web in apps/web and API in services/api. Share config with team, but do not start anything.
evals.expected_output: Inspect existing registration and repo commands, create or minimally edit root solo.yml with relative working directories and explicit safe defaults, explain sync/trust handoff, and avoid runtime or Git side effects.
assertions: Uses or proposes root solo.yml only after checking existing project/config
Sets auto_start and auto_restart explicitly, defaulting false unless requested
Uses relative in-root working_dir values and excludes secrets
Does not start processes, commit, push, publish, or integrate

evals.prompt: Can you add this folder to Solo?
evals.expected_output: Resolve folder/project scope, check for canonical duplicate and existing solo.yml, register only if needed, then report any trust or UI setup step without inventing commands or launching them.
assertions: Checks list_projects before create_project
Handles ambiguous folder or project scope safely
Does not assume solo.yml is required
Does not auto-run detected or shared commands

evals.prompt: The API server is down. Restart its Solo process and tell me when port 3000 is ready.
evals.expected_output: Route lifecycle control to solo-run-processes and readiness observation to solo-observe-services; do not edit project configuration as a substitute.
assertions: Recognizes request as neighboring lifecycle/readiness work
Does not edit solo.yml or project settings
Requires authority for YAML-backed shared process
Avoids unrelated setup side effects

evals.prompt: $solo-set-up-projects for my new backend repo: define build, test, and migrate commands in solo.yml with correct working directories.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-set-up-projects to inspect existing registration, then add build, test, and migrate commands to root solo.yml with relative working directories and explicit safe defaults, without launching them.
assertions: Checks existing project and solo.yml before editing
Defines commands with relative working_dir values in shared solo.yml
Sets auto_start and auto_restart explicitly, defaulting false
Does not run the defined commands

evals.prompt: hey can you get this repo wired up in Solo so the whole team shares the same commands?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-set-up-projects to register the repository and capture shared commands in root solo.yml for team sync, explaining the trust handoff without starting anything.
assertions: Calls list_projects before create_project
Places shared commands in root solo.yml for sync
Explains trust review handoff
Does not auto-run shared commands

evals.prompt: Set up environment values and a staging execution profile for the build command in this Solo project.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-set-up-projects to add the execution profile and non-secret environment values to the build command's project configuration, keeping secrets out of shared solo.yml.
assertions: Configures execution profile in project config
Excludes secrets from shared solo.yml
Keeps machine-specific values local
Does not start the build command

evals.prompt: Register this monorepo in Solo, set auto-restart on the API command and file-watch restart on web, but don't launch anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-set-up-projects to register the monorepo and set the auto-restart and file-watch restart policies in configuration, deferring all process launch.
assertions: Registers project only after checking list_projects
Sets restart policies explicitly in configuration
Honors the no-launch constraint
Does not start or spawn any process

evals.prompt: Import the existing solo.yml from this repo into Solo and walk me through the trust review before any command runs.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-set-up-projects to import the repo's solo.yml, reconcile it with existing registration, and describe the trust review gate without executing commands.
assertions: Imports and syncs the repo solo.yml
Reconciles with existing project registration
Surfaces trust review before command execution
Does not run imported commands

evals.prompt: Add lint and typecheck commands to this project's shared config and set their working directories to packages/core.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-set-up-projects to add lint and typecheck commands to root solo.yml with packages/core as the relative working directory and safe defaults.
assertions: Adds commands to shared solo.yml
Uses relative in-root working_dir values
Sets safe auto_start and auto_restart defaults
Does not execute lint or typecheck

evals.prompt: I want this repo in Solo with its command definitions stored locally, not shared. Set that up.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-set-up-projects to register the repository and store its command definitions in local project state rather than shared solo.yml, per the requested command storage scope.
assertions: Registers project after checking existing state
Honors local command storage over shared solo.yml
Keeps machine-specific state local
Does not launch stored commands

evals.prompt: Switch Solo to a dark theme and rearrange my sidebar and window layout.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-set-up-projects; route to $solo-customize-workspace for app-level appearance and layout personalization.
assertions: Recognizes personal workspace UI, not project setup
Routes to solo-customize-workspace
Does not touch solo.yml or project registration
Avoids process side effects

evals.prompt: Remap the command palette shortcut and change my Solo notification settings.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-set-up-projects; route to $solo-customize-workspace for keymaps and notification preferences.
assertions: Recognizes keymap and notification personalization
Routes to solo-customize-workspace
Does not edit project or command configuration
Makes no runtime changes

evals.prompt: Connect my external editor to Solo and update my account display name.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-set-up-projects; route to $solo-customize-workspace for external tool integration and account display.
assertions: Recognizes external tool and account display scope
Routes to solo-customize-workspace
Does not register repositories or edit solo.yml
Avoids process or Git side effects

evals.prompt: Start the dev and API commands now.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-set-up-projects; route to $solo-run-processes for command lifecycle control.
assertions: Recognizes runtime process control
Routes to solo-run-processes
Does not edit solo.yml or project settings
Requires authority for YAML-backed shared commands

evals.prompt: Restart the web process and send 'rs' to the running dev command.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-set-up-projects; route to $solo-run-processes for restart and process input.
assertions: Recognizes restart and input as lifecycle work
Routes to solo-run-processes
Does not modify project configuration
Avoids unrelated setup side effects

evals.prompt: My solo.yml edits aren't taking effect and the command still fails. Figure out why.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-set-up-projects; route to $solo-troubleshoot for solo.yml sync and command failure diagnosis.
assertions: Recognizes failure diagnosis, not new setup
Routes to solo-troubleshoot
Does not rewrite configuration blindly
Investigates sync and command failure first

evals.prompt: Solo shows the project as untrusted and status disagrees with what's actually running. Diagnose it.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-set-up-projects; route to $solo-troubleshoot for trust and status-discrepancy diagnosis.
assertions: Recognizes trust and status discrepancy as diagnosis
Routes to solo-troubleshoot
Does not re-register or reconfigure as a fix
Avoids runtime side effects

evals.prompt: Is the API server ready yet, and what is it listening on for port 3000?
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-set-up-projects; route to $solo-observe-services for read-only status, readiness, and port inspection.
assertions: Recognizes read-only status and readiness query
Routes to solo-observe-services
Does not edit project configuration
Changes no runtime state
